free_text: Feeding of Rh and Ag isomers in fast-neutron-induced
US Army Research Laboratory, Adelphi, Maryland
spallation neutron source on the 60R flight path.
The gamma rays produced in the bombardment of
the 103Rh and 109Ag targets by neutrons were measured
with the GEANIE spectrometer. GEANIE comprised 10
coaxial Ge detectors. Several HPGe detectors were not
used for analysis because of insufficient energy
resolution or frequent gain shift during experiment.
Energy of incident neutrons was determined
using time-of-flight method. The beam time structure
consisted of 625 mu-s long macropulses at 40 Hz rate.
micropulses spaced every 1.8 mu-s. At this
overlap of high-energy neutrons of one pulse with
lower-energy neutrons of the previous pulse start at
The neutrons were produced in a natural W
spallation target driven by an 800 MeV proton beam.
